summary: Modernizing distributed service architecture, owning high-availability operations, and automating infrastructure changes for faster, cheaper, and more reliable delivery.
impact: Modernized service architectures by implementing hexagonal architecture principles, improving maintainability and testability and driving a 70% improvement in deployment velocity by eliminating recurring pipeline bottlenecks and integration test failures.

company: University of Illinois College of Medicine
summary: Built secure, scalable research software for an NIH-funded healthcare platform handling confidential patient data, complex study workflows, and large neurosurgical datasets.
impact: Collaborated on an NIH-funded project to develop a secure, scalable, and HIPAA-compliant platform for efficient management and analysis of confidential patient health information.
Built a dynamic form builder enabling users to define instruments with configurable fields, validation rules, and privacy flags for sensitive data, streamlining research study setup and data capture.

Engineered a resumable, asynchronous file-upload pipeline for cumulative uploads up to roughly 10 GB with checksum verification and automatic retry logic, reducing failures and cutting user wait times by about 60%.
Shipped a high-performance EEG viewer with persistent annotation support for multi-channel neurosurgical data, capable of rendering more than 10 million data points in real time.
notes: This entry intentionally compresses four adjacent UIC roles into one cleaner timeline anchor to avoid clutter.
